info.name: GitHub Profile Analyzer API
info.description:
Complete test suite for the GitHub Profile Analyzer REST API.

## Setup
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your server URL
2. Set the `apiKey` variable to your API secret key
3. After running 'Analyze Profile', copy the `githubId` from the response and set the `githubId` variable

## Endpoints
- POST /api/profiles/analyze — Analyze a GitHub username
- GET  /api/profiles       — List profiles (paginated, filterable)
- GET  /api/profiles/:id   — Get single profile + insights
- PUT  /api/profiles/:id/refresh — Re-analyze existing profile
- DELETE /api/profiles/:id — Delete profile and insights
- GET  /health             — Service health check

variable.description: Change to http://localhost:3000 for local development
Your API_SECRET_KEY value from the .env file or Render dashboard
GitHub numeric user ID — set this after running 'Analyze Profile'

request.description: Check API and database health. No authentication required. Returns service status and DB latency.

Fetches a GitHub user profile and their public repositories, computes insights (stars, forks, language distribution, most starred/forked repo, open source license count, etc.), and persists all data in MySQL.

Returns 201 on first analysis, 200 on re-analysis.

Rate limit: 20 requests per 15 minutes per IP.
Returns a paginated list of all analyzed profiles with their insights embedded.

query.description: Sort field: lastAnalyzedAt | followerCount | totalStarsEarned | accountCreationDate

request.description: Filter profiles by top programming language, sorted by follower count descending.

item.name: List Profiles — Filter by Followers & Date

request.description: Filter profiles by follower range and account creation date, sorted by total stars.

Retrieves the full profile metadata and all computed insights for a single user by their GitHub numeric ID.

Set the `githubId` collection variable to the ID returned from the Analyze endpoint.

Re-fetches the latest data from GitHub for an existing stored profile. Invalidates the LRU cache for this user before fetching to guarantee fresh data. Updates all insight fields in the database using a transaction.

Permanently deletes a stored profile and all its associated insights. The CASCADE constraint on the database foreign key ensures the profile_insights row is also deleted automatically.

Returns 204 No Content on success.
Opens the interactive Swagger UI (OpenAPI 3.0) in the browser. All endpoints, request schemas, and response schemas are documented here.
